_schema_note: jpods-map-v2 — 2026-05-20 rev 2. Full physical network document. Written by Noelle on every Build/Validate. One source of truth for all consumers: SketchUp visualizer, TripPlanner, Nora (Pi), Alice (billing), MeshMobility, Dispatcher. All distances in mm. All coordinates in mm (x=east, y=north, z=up from model origin). Converges SketchUp and podPresenter formats. Deliberation: 2026-05-20. Field renames applied: stub→cp_index. Added: followme_hash, last_physical_survey, nominal_time_s per line, stations block, ezone_status.
_note_followme_hash: SHA-256 of the followme.json file this map was derived from. Noelle writes at Build time. Nora and TripPlanner compare against the followme.json hash at dispatch — if different, the map is stale and must not be used. null means not yet computed (draft).
_note_last_physical_survey: ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent complete Nora traversal that confirmed physical.json is current for all lines. Null if no survey has been run. Dispatcher can block trip assignments if this is too old or null.
_innovation_global: PROMOTED — followme_hash: required field at top level. Safety: stale map detection. Noelle writes SHA at Build.
PROMOTED — last_physical_survey: required field. Dispatcher uses to age-gate physical.json. Nora writes after full survey run.
PROMOTED — nominal_time_s per line: required field on every line. MeshMobility reads; never recalculates fixed geometry.
PROMOTED — stations block: top-level index of all stations with capacity and template. Dispatcher and Alice read; Noelle writes at Build.
PROMOTED — ezone_status on each ezone: runtime field set by Dispatcher. Nora reads before entry.
OPEN — ALLIE: what cross-domain data belongs at the map level vs. line level? (solar capacity, CO2 offset, cargo throughput ceiling)
OPEN — ALLIE: should map.json carry solar_panel_count per line (not just network_summary) so Alice can calculate per-trip solar offset credit?

stations._note: Top-level station index. Noelle writes at Build from followme.json models and noelle_features.json. Dispatcher reads to check platform availability before assigning trips. Alice reads for fare zone lookup.

S048._note_template: Component definition name from noelle_features.json. Drives platform capacity, dwell_time_s, allowed segment sequences.
S048._note_platform_capacity: Max pods on gw_platform simultaneously. Derived from length_mm / nominal_pod_length_mm by Noelle.
S048._note_cp_count: Number of stub_pairs (connection points) on this station. Noelle counts from followme.json.

lines._note: Every guideway segment — intra-station (gw_*) and inter-station (seg_*) — is a complete self-contained record. Consumers never re-derive geometry from SketchUp entities. Key: same id used in trip.json segments. Distances mm. Coordinates mm.

S048.gw_platform._note: Intra-station line. Role = gw_platform means this is the loading/unloading track. Pods dwell here for passenger exchange.
S048.gw_platform._note_nominal_time_s: Nominal travel time = length_mm / speedMax (mm/s). MeshMobility reads; never recalculates. Noelle computes at Build from speedMax.

segments._note: grade_pct = dz/len * 100. Zero here — flat platform. Uphill positive, downhill negative. Nora adjusts motor power by grade.

S048.gw_platform._note_markers: AprilTag markers on this line. Each marker: {id, dist_mm, x_mm, y_mm, z_mm, value}. dist_mm = distance from startPoint where marker is mounted on guideway structure. Physical Nora reads AprilTag value to confirm position.

seg_S048_cp1_S050_cp0._note: Inter-station line. Bezier path with waypoints — multiple segments. grade_pct per segment captures vertical profile for Nora motor control and MeshMobility energy model.
seg_S048_cp1_S050_cp0._note_cp_index: cp_index is the stub_pair number (0-based) on this station. Renamed from 'stub' (deliberation 2026-05-20). Matches noelle_features.json stub_pair numbering.

segments._note: Segments subdivide the Bezier path at ~2m intervals. Populated by Noelle from followme.json track geometry.

seg_S048_cp1_S050_cp0._note_segments: Full segment array omitted from draft — Noelle generates from followme.json path data at Build time. Each 2m step becomes one segment record.

markers._note: Waypoint marker 1 on this connection. Physical: AprilTag mounted on guideway column. dist_mm from startPoint.
markers._note_xy: x/y/z in mm from model origin — Noelle populates from marker entity position at Build time.

energy_model._note: Per-line energy estimate. MeshMobility and Alice use this. Noelle calculates from grade profile + length. Physical Nora logs actual vs. estimated for calibration.

ezones._note: Merge/diverge points where collision risk exists. Nora must check ezone clearance before entering. Same structure as podPresenter — converged.
ezones._note_ezone_status: Runtime field. Values: clear | occupied | blocked. Dispatcher sets before Nora enters. Nora reads; if not 'clear', holds and retries. Map.json carries last-known state; Dispatcher owns writes.
ezones._note_approach_decel_mm: Distance before ezone entry where Nora begins deceleration ramp. Derived from ezMaxSpeed and normal speed. Noelle calculates at Build.

dead_end_nodes._note: Lines that terminate without a successor — line-end stations. Nora must stop before these. Noelle flags them.

network_summary._note: Top-level network stats. Alice uses for capacity planning. MeshMobility uses for network-wide metrics. Noelle calculates at Build.
network_summary._note_solar: Noelle counts solar panel component instances at Build. Alice uses for energy offset calculation.
